subject: Databases
title: Hash Indexes
description: A quick, practical guide to Hash Indexes.

hook.text: WHERE email = 'ada@test.com' is a pure equality check. A hash index handles this in O(1) time.

buildup.text: Hash indexes map keys to buckets using a hash function. Lookup is direct — no tree traversal needed.

discovery.text: They're faster than B-trees for exact equality. But they can't handle ranges: WHERE age > 18 won't work.

twist.text: Postgres hash indexes weren't crash-safe until version 10. Before that, they were rarely used in production.

climax.text: Use hash indexes for exact lookups on large text columns where B-tree overhead is high.

punchline.text: Hash: fast equality, no ranges. Use B-tree for everything else.

quiz.question: Why can't hash indexes support range queries?
options: Hash functions destroy ordering, so there's no way to find values in a range
Hash indexes can only store numbers
Range queries are not supported by any index type
